parts.text:
You are working in /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe on branch ISS-24/allow-list-connections.

## Task
Add `AllowedHosts []string` to the `ExecContext` struct in `internal/tool/registry.go`.

## Step 1: Read the current `ExecContext` struct

Read `internal/tool/registry.go` and find the `ExecContext` struct (around line 13). It currently has:
```go
type ExecContext struct {
    Workdir string
    Stderr  io.Writer
    Verbose bool
}
```

## Step 2: Add the field

Add `AllowedHosts []string` to the struct:
```go
type ExecContext struct {
    Workdir      string
    Stderr       io.Writer
    Verbose      bool
    AllowedHosts []string
}
```

## Step 3: Verify no compilation errors

Run `cd /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe && go build ./...` to confirm the entire project still compiles. Adding a field to a struct should not break anything since Go allows partial struct literal initialization.

## Step 4: Run all tool tests to verify no regressions

Run `cd /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe && go test ./internal/tool/` to confirm all existing tests still pass.

## What to return
Return:
1. The exact change you made (before/after)
2. The build output
3. The test output

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/registry.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"io"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
9: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
10: )
11: 
12: // ExecContext holds the minimal context needed by generic tool executors.
13: type ExecContext struct {
14: 	Workdir string
15: 	Stderr  io.Writer
16: 	Verbose bool
17: }
18: 
19: // ToolEntry holds a tool's definition and executor functions.
20: type ToolEntry struct {
21: 	Definition func() provider.Tool
22: 	Execute    func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult
23: }
24: 
25: // Registry maps tool names to their definitions and executors.
26: // Registration must happen at startup before any concurrent Dispatch calls.
27: // Concurrent Dispatch calls (read-only) are safe after registration is complete.
28: type Registry struct {
29: 	entries map[string]ToolEntry
30: }
31: 
32: // NewRegistry returns a new Registry with an empty, initialized entries map.
33: func NewRegistry() *Registry {
34: 	return &Registry{
35: 		entries: make(map[string]ToolEntry),
36: 	}
37: }
38: 
39: // Register adds a tool entry to the registry, keyed by name.
40: // If a tool with the same name already exists, it is silently replaced.

(Showing lines 1-40 of 100. Use offset=41 to continue.)
</content>
